mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in the 3-month moving average of non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a potential stall in the economic recovery. This leads to downward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts and causes negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The May 2016 jobs report released in early June was significantly weaker than expected.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in over 50% of instances where the 3-month moving average of PAYEMS decelerates by more than 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in year-over-year retail sales growth signals that consumer spending, the primary engine of the US economy, is faltering. This leads to negative returns as forward earnings estimates are revised down.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change of RSAFS falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an upside surprise in the year-over-year Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) increases the market-implied probability of more aggressive future rate hikes, compressing equity valuation multiples.
candidate_trigger: YoY CPIAUCSL is positive and FEDFUNDS has increased in the prior 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve initiated its first rate hike in December 2015.
failure_condition: The short-term correlation between daily CPIAUCSL surprises and S&P 500 returns is non-negative during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low and non-farm payroll (PAYEMS) growth falters, it signals that the labor market has reached peak employment and is beginning to roll over, a classic late-cycle indicator preceding recessions and bear markets.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 4.7% and recent PAYEMS growth has been weak as of June 2016.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 has a positive median 60-day forward return over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained negative year-over-year growth in Industrial Production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and goods-producing sector. This is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate profits and equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:There was a notable 'industrial recession' in the US during 2015-2016.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year rank correlation between INDPRO YoY growth and 60-day S&P 500 forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month percentage drop in housing starts (HOUST) signals a sharp decline in residential investment, a highly cyclical and interest-rate sensitive sector. This weakness is a leading indicator for broader economic downturns.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average return on non-trigger days (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reflects a tightening of monetary policy. This directly increases corporate borrowing costs and raises the discount rate applied to future earnings, putting downward pressure on equity valuations.
failure_condition: A regression of 20-day S&P 500 forward returns on the 1-month change in FEDFUNDS yields a positive and statistically significant coefficient over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A downward revision to the prior quarter's real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals that underlying economic momentum was weaker than previously understood. This forces a systematic downward reassessment of future growth and earnings expectations, negatively impacting equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is a high-revision series, and markets react to changes in the estimated growth path.
failure_condition: The market's reaction to GDP revisions shows no statistically significant negative bias; returns are positive in >50% of instances of downward revisions.
